req.title: System-Admin views performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a performance-review through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
preconditions: authenticated
role:admin
performance-review module is enabled

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-024 requires an authenticated system-admin to view a performance-review via GET /api/performance-reviews/{id}. Test design covers: (1) EP positive – valid admin token retrieves an existing review and audit record is created; (2) NEG – unauthenticated request returns 401; (3) NEG/DT – authenticated non-admin role returns 403; (4) BVA – requesting a review with a boundary ID value (minimum valid integer = 1) returns 200; (5) STC – requesting a deleted/non-existent review transitions from 'active' to 'not-found' state and returns 404, confirming state-dependent behaviour. Together these cover all stated error pathways and the success path with audit validation.

tests.name: test_admin_views_existing_performance_review_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: System is running and /api/performance-reviews is reachable
A valid system-admin JWT token is available via <admin_token fixture>
A seeded performance-review record exists via <seeded_review fixture>
Performance-review module is enabled
expected: Response body contains all mandatory fields (employee_id, period, rating)
Audit log contains one entry for the view action on this entity id
trace: R-WEB-024: Expected outcomes – performance-review is viewed; response 200; audit record written
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_admin_headers, assert_audit_entry

def test_admin_views_existing_performance_review_returns_200_and_audit(
    admin_token,        # <fixture>
    seeded_review,      # <fixture> returns dict with 'id'
    api_client,         # <fixture> wraps requests.Session with base_url
):
    review_id = seeded_review["id"]
    headers = get_admin_headers(admin_token)

    resp = api_client.get(f"/api/performance-reviews/{review_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["id"] == review_id
    for field in ("employee_id", "period", "rating"):
        assert field in body, f"Mandatory field '{field}' missing"

    assert_audit_entry(
        api_client=api_client,
        headers=headers,
        entity="performance-review",
        entity_id=review_id,
        action="VIEW",
    )

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
preconditions: System is running and /api/performance-reviews is reachable
No Authorization header is provided
Performance-review module is enabled
expected: Response body contains an error or message field indicating unauthenticated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401(
    api_client,     # <fixture>
    seeded_review,  # <fixture> ensures a real id exists
):
    review_id = seeded_review["id"]

    resp = api_client.get(f"/api/performance-reviews/{review_id}")

    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_returns_403
preconditions: System is running and /api/performance-reviews is reachable
A valid JWT token for a non-admin user (role: viewer) is available via <viewer_token fixture>
A seeded performance-review record exists via <seeded_review fixture>
Performance-review module is enabled
expected: Response body contains error indicating forbidden/insufficient role
trace: R-WEB-024: Error pathways – 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_bearer_headers

def test_non_admin_role_returns_403(
    viewer_token,   # <fixture> returns JWT for role:viewer
    seeded_review,  # <fixture>
    api_client,     # <fixture>
):
    review_id = seeded_review["id"]
    headers = get_bearer_headers(viewer_token)

    resp = api_client.get(f"/api/performance-reviews/{review_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_admin_views_review_with_minimum_boundary_id_returns_200
preconditions: System is running and /api/performance-reviews is reachable
A valid system-admin JWT token is available via <admin_token fixture>
A performance-review seeded with the lowest assigned integer ID (boundary_review fixture) exists
Performance-review module is enabled
actions: GET /api/performance-reviews/{min_id} where min_id is the minimum existing review ID
Assert response and mandatory field presence
expected: Response body id equals the minimum boundary ID
All mandatory fields present in response body
trace: R-WEB-024: BVA on entity identifier – minimum valid ID must be retrievable
R-WEB-024: Expected outcomes – response 200 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_admin_headers

def test_admin_views_review_with_minimum_boundary_id_returns_200(
    admin_token,        # <fixture>
    boundary_review,    # <fixture> seeds & returns review with smallest int id
    api_client,         # <fixture>
):
    min_id = boundary_review["id"]
    headers = get_admin_headers(admin_token)

    resp = api_client.get(f"/api/performance-reviews/{min_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["id"] == min_id
    for field in ("employee_id", "period", "rating"):
        assert field in body, f"Mandatory field '{field}' missing at boundary id"

tests.name: test_admin_views_deleted_review_transitions_to_not_found
preconditions: System is running and /api/performance-reviews is reachable
A valid system-admin JWT token is available via <admin_token fixture>
A performance-review exists and is then soft-deleted via <deleted_review fixture>
Performance-review module is enabled
actions: Seed a review in state ACTIVE via <deleted_review fixture>
Fixture transitions the review to state DELETED via DELETE /api/performance-reviews/{id}
expected: Response body contains error indicating resource not found
No audit record for a VIEW action is created for this request
trace: R-WEB-024: STC – ACTIVE -> DELETED state transition; deleted entity must not be viewable
R-WEB-024: Error pathways – implicit 404 for non-existent/deleted entities
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_admin_headers, count_audit_entries

def test_admin_views_deleted_review_transitions_to_not_found(
    admin_token,      # <fixture>
    deleted_review,   # <fixture> creates then deletes review; returns dict with 'id'
    api_client,       # <fixture>
):
    review_id = deleted_review["id"]
    headers = get_admin_headers(admin_token)

    resp = api_client.get(f"/api/performance-reviews/{review_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 404
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

    audit_count = count_audit_entries(
        api_client=api_client,
        headers=headers,
        entity="performance-review",
        entity_id=review_id,
        action="VIEW",
    )
    assert audit_count == 0, "No VIEW audit entry expected for deleted review"
